blurb: Highlights include a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire transition and a powerful Playing In The Band sandwich. Brent Mydland’s fresh organ adds energy to Wharf Rat, while the rhythm section drives the second set. Overall performance is solid with a few uneven moments in the first set.
review: October 24, 1979 at the Springfield Civic Center captures a late‑summer Dead that was still finding its groove but delivered several memorable peaks. The second set is the show’s engine room – a blistering Scarlet Begonias into Fire on the Mountain segues seamlessly into a thunderous Playing In The Band sandwich, punctuated by an eleven‑minute drum/space excursion that launches into an expansive Wharf Rat. Brent Mydland, barely a year into his tenure, injects swirling B‑3 organ textures and fresh vocal harmonies, especially on Wharf Rat and Samson & Delilah, while the twin drummers lay down heavy, funk‑laden grooves that keep the momentum rolling. Jerry’s gritty guitar tone shines throughout, though he slips a lyric or two on “Loser” and “Sailor > Saint”. Phil’s bass provides a steady undercurrent, and Bob’s vocals hold firm on the high‑energy numbers. The first set has its rough spots – “Around And Around” falls flat and the early songs feel like a warm‑up – but the band’s chemistry solidifies as the night progresses, making this a worthwhile listen for fans of the era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings note strong moments (Scarlet>Fire, Playing In The Band sandwich, Wharf Rat) and praise Brent’s fresh energy, while only a few mixed comments mention uneven first‑set spots; no consistent negative criticism of the band’s playing, yielding a modest positive sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape with vivid crowd ambience, solid sound and the standout Playing In The Band sandwich captured clearly.

key_highlights: Smoking Playing In The Band sandwich with extended drum/space jam
Heavy organ swells and trippy vibe on Wharf Rat
Samson & Delilah delivers a powerful, gritty performance

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping, gritty guitar tone throughout; solid on the second set with a few lyric flubs early on.
band_performance.Phil: Bass lines drive the improvisations, especially during the jam sections, providing a firm foundation.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal performance strong on Playing In The Band and balances rhythm duties in the second‑set flow.
band_performance.Brent: Fresh energy with swirling B3 organ on Wharf Rat and Samson & Delilah; early era funk‑organ praised across recordings.
band_performance.Mickey: Heavy, funk‑laden drumming fuels the extended drum/space segment and keeps the groove tight.
band_performance.Billy: Partners with Mickey to deliver powerful rhythmic drive that underpins the second set’s high energy.
